laws.title: Public Law 29-208 | Foreign Affairs Accountability Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Requires monthly ambassador reporting, a State Department summary/report duty, and a major-international-developments report duty; repeals Section IV of Public Law 15-102.
laws.provision_level_disposition: Standalone general and permanent law preserved as a statutory note at 22 U.S.C. 2658a; no uniquely executable positive-law amendment was identified.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is intact and permanent, and no later enactment in the reviewed set repeals Public Law 29-208.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Section IV of Public Law 15-102 is repealed; no transfer or redesignation chain survives.
laws.status_basis: The source text is readable and permanent, but it does not produce a uniquely executable Code substitution. The mass-migration record classifies it as a statutory note at 22 U.S.C. 2658a.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML already carries the Pub. L. 29-208 statutory note at the title 22 anchor.

source_evidence: codification/laws/laws/PL-029-208/law.txt states the monthly reporting duties and the repeal of Section IV of Public Law 15-102.
codification/mass_migration/latest/reports/laws/PL-029-208.md classifies the act as STATUTORY_NOTE at 22 U.S.C. 2658a.
usc/usc22.xml contains the note id rp-pl029208-codification at the title 22 anchor.
recommended_actions: Retain the statutory note at 22 U.S.C. 2658a.
Do not create a new Code section.
Keep the repeal history for Public Law 15-102 section IV.

laws.title: Public Law 29-210 | Military Justice Reform Act of 2024
laws.final_statutory_text: Creates a freestanding military justice structure, amends multiple UCMJ article provisions by reference to Public Law 14-91, repeals Public Laws 15-104 and 19-146, and includes blanket supersession language.
laws.provision_level_disposition: General and permanent military-justice enactment preserved as a statutory note; no safe concrete Title 10 section-body target was determinable from the packet text.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is intact and reviewable, and no later enactment in the reviewed set repeals Public Law 29-210.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Direct repeal of Public Laws 15-104 and 19-146, with blanket supersession language and no safe transfer/redesignation chain.
laws.status_basis: The packet text is complete and reviewable, but the review memo found no safely determinable current XML target for a concrete section-body rewrite.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML carries the Pub. L. 29-210 statutory note, but no definitive section-body substitution was safely authored.

source_evidence: codification/decisions/PL-029-210.md says there is no present XML operation and no safely determinable current XML target.
usc/usc10.xml contains the note id rp-pl029210-codification.
codification/plans/draft/PL-029-210.json remains a zero-operation draft.
recommended_actions: Preserve the statutory note under title 10.
Do not infer a concrete section-body substitution without a formal policy mapping.
Do not treat the blanket supersession clause as a free-standing XML rewrite instruction.

laws.title: Public Law 29-211 | Amendment to the Presidential Succession Act of 1947
laws.final_statutory_text: Amends 3 U.S.C. 19(c)(2), (d)(1), and (d)(2) to revise the acting-presidency succession rules.
laws.provision_level_disposition: Direct amendment to existing Code text; implemented through the title 3 section-19 fragment.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is intact and operative, and later laws reviewed for this section do not undo the amendment.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer chain; the act is an affirmative amendment to 3 U.S.C. 19.
laws.status_basis: The decision memorandum and approved fragment both identify a direct section-19 amendment, and the current branch contains the corresponding amendment note.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML contains the Pub. L. 29-211 amendment note and the revised 3 U.S.C. 19 subsections.

source_evidence: codification/decisions/PL-029-211.md says section 3 amends 3 U.S.C. 19(c)(2), (d)(1), and (d)(2).
usc/usc03.xml contains the note id rp-pl029211-3-19-amendments.
codification/plans/approved/PL-029-211-t3-s19-fragment.xml is the approved replacement fragment.
recommended_actions: Retain the current 3 U.S.C. 19 text and history note.
No further Code change is required for this law.
Keep the amendment history aligned with Public Law 29-215.

laws.title: Public Law 29-213 | D.C Delegate Elimination Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Repeals Public Law 16-120 section 4 and declares the delegate position null and void.

laws.title: Public Law 29-214 | Safeguarding American Democracy and Maintaining Order in our Streets Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Prohibits unauthorized private paramilitary activity, defines the covered conduct, and sets exceptions and penalties.

laws.title: Public Law 29-215 | Stability in the Presidency Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Adds 3 U.S.C. 19(g) and (h) governing incapacity declarations, appeal, congressional action, and judicial review.
laws.provision_level_disposition: Direct amendment to existing Code text; implemented through the title 3 section-19 fragment.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is intact and operative, and later laws reviewed for this section do not undo the amendment.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: No repeal or transfer chain; the act adds new subsections to 3 U.S.C. 19.
laws.status_basis: The decision memorandum and current XML both show the new subsections (g) and (h) added to 3 U.S.C. 19.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML contains the added subsections (g) and (h) and the Pub. L. 29-215 amendment note.

recommended_actions: Retain the current 3 U.S.C. 19 text and history note.

laws.title: Public Law 29-217 | Private Regulations and Compliance Act

laws.title: Public Law 30-220 | Supreme Court Justice Allotment Act
laws.final_statutory_text: Reinstates and amends 28 U.S.C. 42 to authorize allotment of Supreme Court justices as circuit justices or district judges and allows repeated or shared assignments.
laws.provision_level_disposition: Direct amendment to existing Code text; implemented through the title 28 section-42 replacement and TOC update.
laws.chronology: Current enactment. The source is intact and operative, and the later-law review found no undoing repeal.
laws.repeal_transfer_redesignation: Public Law 17-133 previously repealed the section, but Public Law 30-220 independently reinstates and amends it; no non-revival issue blocks implementation.
laws.status_basis: The decision memorandum approves reinstatement and amendment of 28 U.S.C. 42, and the current branch contains the amended section text and history note.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML contains the reinstated section text, the updated heading, and the Pub. L. 30-220 amendment note.

source_evidence: codification/decisions/PL-030-220.md says Public Law 30-220 reinstates and amends 28 U.S.C. 42.
usc/usc28.xml contains the section 42 text and the Pub. L. 30-220, § 3, amendment note.
codification/packets/PL-030-220/law.txt states the allotment authority for circuit justices and district judges.
recommended_actions: Retain the amended 28 U.S.C. 42 text and history note.
Keep the updated title 28 table of contents heading.
No further Code change is required for this law.
